summary: Editing a customer's dozens is now permanent and no longer reverts after the weekly run resets.
highlights: Fixing dozens for a customer on the route edit screen now sticks across weeks instead of reverting to the original import value.
The app stores the edited value as the new default so the weekly reset uses the updated number going forward.
CSV and diagnostic file downloads are more reliable on iPhone and Safari.

summary: Restore now repairs invalid Dozens safely and gives a review screen before finalizing edits.
highlights: Restore now warns when backup rows contain invalid or blank Dozens values and asks before applying fallback repairs.
When fallback repair runs, the app downloads a restore-repair JSON report so support can inspect exactly what was corrected.
Home now shows a temporary Review repaired rows panel after restore so you can adjust repaired Dozens and apply those updates in-app.
Backup export now writes delivery Dozens from current delivery data when old import-state Dozens cells are blank, preventing repeat restore failures.
Release waiver: Full TP-01 through TP-11 manual/device checks and usage-scenario checks were skipped for this release (approved by repo owner on 2026-02-15).

summary: Home backup buttons now fit reliably on small screens.
highlights: Import CSV, Backup CSV, and Restore CSV now use a responsive grid layout instead of a forced single row.
Button labels can wrap cleanly so larger text settings and narrow phones do not overflow horizontally.
On small screens, actions automatically reflow to 2-column and then 1-column layouts for readability and tap safety.
Release waiver: Full TP-01 through TP-11 manual/device checks and usage-scenario checks were skipped for this release (approved by repo owner on 2026-02-15).

summary: Permanent route-order guardrails are live and export diagnostics are much deeper.
highlights: Route Planner now blocks reorder actions in All Schedules view so global route order cannot be rewritten there.
Order edits now only reorder within a single schedule and use canonical storage order when saving.
Storage now rejects sort-order writes with missing schedule rows, duplicate deliveries, mixed schedules, or out-of-schedule IDs.
Diagnostic JSON exports now include app/build metadata, runtime environment, export attempt context, and route order integrity summaries.
The update popup is now high-visibility at the top of the app with stronger copy and contrast.
The temporary Home Repair button was removed after permanent guardrails shipped.
Release waiver: Full TP-01 through TP-11 manual/device checks and usage-scenario checks were skipped for this release (approved by repo owner on 2026-02-15).

summary: Export is fixed on iPhone and Home now has a one-tap route order repair tool.
highlights: Fixed iPhone/Safari export failure caused by a Navigator.canShare binding error.
Export now safely falls back to direct download if file-share capability checks fail.
Home now includes a Repair button that restores route order from run-history snapshots after confirmation.
Route repair only applies when a usable dense snapshot exists; otherwise it skips instead of guessing.
Release waiver: Full TP-01 through TP-11 manual/device checks and usage-scenario checks were skipped for this release (approved by repo owner on 2026-02-15).

summary: Backups are more reliable and route ordering now protects data integrity.
highlights: Backup export now falls back to direct CSV download if sharing fails.
Export failures now show specific error details instead of a generic failure message.
When CSV export fails, the app attempts a diagnostic JSON snapshot export.
Editing order from All Schedules no longer rewrites order across other schedules.
Storage now rejects mixed-schedule reorder writes to prevent accidental global reindexing.
Home now includes a Repair button that restores route order from run-history snapshots after a confirmation prompt.
Release process note: Manual TP/device and usage-scenario checks were waived by the repo owner on 2026-02-15.

summary: Build info is more reliable and reports when it can’t be loaded.
highlights: Home footer now labels the installed build time.
The app checks for updates on launch and when it becomes active.
If an update is ready, use the prompt to reload to the newest build.
Build info uses a freshness-first cache strategy.
A fallback message appears when build info can’t be fetched.

summary: Home now includes a Release Info button with recent updates.
highlights: Release Info shows the latest updates in plain language.
Updates are ordered newest-first and available offline.
Update available prompt lets you reload to the newest version.
Keep screen awake is temporarily hidden on iPhone while support improves.

summary: Donation entry is smoother and now accepts five-digit amounts.
highlights: Custom donation amounts stay when you choose Cash, ACH, Venmo, or PayPal.
Donations up to 99,999 are now accepted.

summary: Receipts and planner totals are clearer and more reliable.
highlights: Receipt history totals match one-off donations more consistently.
Planner receipt edits have clearer validation.

summary: Backup/restore and run history tracking improved.
highlights: Backup and restore flows are more reliable.
Run history exports include more complete details.
Reliability updates across core screens.
Minor copy and layout polish.
